reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
It's the Monday before Memorial Day. A lot of people are pacing toward the long weekend like it's a finish line — patio plans, lake houses, the kind of stretch I used to organize my whole life around. Back then, holidays weren't days off, they were permission slips. I needed the occasion to justify what I was already going to do anyway.

What I didn't understand at 22 was how much of a life gets built on regular Mondays. Not the big weekends, not the milestones. The unremarkable Tuesday morning where you actually answer your mom's text. The Wednesday workout you didn't feel like doing. The Thursday you went to bed early instead of one more episode. Sobriety isn't a series of dramatic refusals — most days, nobody's offering you anything. It's a series of small repetitions that quietly assemble into a person you recognize in the mirror.

Durant's line about habit gets quoted to death, but it lands different when you've watched your own life rebuild itself one ordinary day at a time. The patio weekend will come. You'll be there, present, remembering it. But the real work is happening right now, on a Monday in May, when nothing is being asked of you except to keep showing up as the person you said you wanted to be.
reflection.meditation: What small, unremarkable action today is actually part of building the life I want?

affirmations: I am building my life in the quiet hours, not just the loud ones.
Today I show up for the ordinary, because that's where my life actually happens.
I have the agency to choose who I'm becoming, one repetition at a time.

gratitude.prayer: I'm grateful for a Monday I get to remember. For the alarm going off and the coffee I actually taste, for the small responsibilities that used to feel like weights and now feel like proof I'm here. There was a long stretch where I couldn't have imagined being grateful for a regular weekday — and that, by itself, is worth sitting with.
gratitude.closing: One ordinary day. That's the whole thing.
